subject: Science
chapter: Changes Around Us: Physical and Chemical

activities_projects.title: The Paper Transformation
activities_projects.objective: Distinguish between a physical and chemical change using paper
steps: Cut the first sheet of paper into small pieces
Burn the second sheet of paper (under adult supervision)
Observe if you can get the original paper back in both cases
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies that cutting is a physical change while burning is a chemical change
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of reversible and irreversible changes (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Ice to Steam Cycle
activities_projects.objective: Observe physical changes in states of matter
steps: Place ice cubes in a bowl and watch them melt into water
Heat the water until it turns into steam
Hold a cold lid over the steam to collect water drops
Explain why this is a physical change
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that change in state is a physical change because no new substance is formed
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct description of the water cycle steps (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sugar Dissolving vs. Charring
activities_projects.objective: Compare a physical process with a chemical reaction
steps: Dissolve a spoon of sugar in water and taste it
Heat a small amount of sugar in a metal cap until it turns black and smells
Note which process created a new substance
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes dissolving as physical and charring as chemical
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate observation of the properties of the end products (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Rusty Nail Watch
steps: Place an iron nail on wet cotton on a plate
Leave it undisturbed for 2-3 days
Observe the brown powdery substance (rust) formed on the surface
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies rusting as a chemical change that creates a new substance
activities_projects.evaluation: Description of the appearance of rust (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Inflating a Balloon
activities_projects.objective: Understand physical change in shape and size
steps: Blow air into a balloon to change its size
Release the air and observe the balloon return to its original state
Discuss if the material of the balloon changed
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns that changes in shape or size without new substance formation are physical

activities_projects.title: Kitchen Indicator Magic
activities_projects.objective: Observe color change as a sign of chemical change
steps: Mix turmeric with water to make yellow paste
Add soap solution and observe it turn red
Add lemon juice to the red spot and watch it turn yellow again
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies color change as an indicator of chemical reactions
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate recording of color transitions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Fruit Browning Experiment
steps: Cut an apple or banana into slices
Leave the slices in the open air for 30 minutes
Observe the change in color of the exposed surface
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that the browning of fruit is a chemical reaction with oxygen
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of why the color changed (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Dough to Roti
activities_projects.objective: Distinguish between reversible and irreversible changes in cooking
steps: Mix flour and water to make dough (Physical change)
Roll it into a circle and then crumble it back (Reversible)
Bake the roti on a hot tawa (Chemical change/Irreversible)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that heat often causes irreversible chemical changes in food
activities_projects.evaluation: Categorizing each step of the cooking process correctly (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Stretching Test
steps: Stretch the rubber band and the spring
Mould the clay into a different shape
Observe that no new material is formed in any of these actions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Reinforces that changes in physical properties are physical changes
activities_projects.evaluation: Verification that the material remains the same (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sorting Game: Change Box
steps: Write 10 examples of changes (e.g., milk turning sour, breaking glass, bursting crackers)
Make two columns: Physical and Chemical
Sort the cards into the correct columns and discuss the reasons
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Consolidates the ability to classify any change correctly
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of sorting the examples (10 marks)
